scenarios.label: Product discovery walkthrough
scenarios.task: Walk the Kitsoki product overview story end to end and explain what Kitsoki does, who it is for, and what the first credible action should be.
success_criteria: Persona can identify the product category without private repo context.
Persona can find a documented next action.
Broken links, stale media, or confusing claims are filed as product findings.

scenarios.label: TUI slash command discovery
scenarios.task: After reading external Codex and Claude Code slash-command docs, use the Kitsoki TUI the same way: type / at the beginning of the prompt, filter the visible command menu, press Tab to accept the primary suggestion, and execute a command successfully.
success_criteria: Typing / at the beginning of the TUI prompt shows available slash commands without requiring Kitsoki-specific docs.
Typing letters after / filters the visible command list to likely matches.
The primary suggestion is visually marked as the Tab completion target.
Pressing Tab accepts the primary suggestion and Enter executes the completed command.

scenarios.label: Project onboarding
scenarios.task: Use the documented Kitsoki onboarding path to configure the assigned repository and explain how bugfix/design work should be launched.

natural_utterances.text: this repo is fresh on this machine - i want make install to run cleanly
so make install will work now?

success_criteria: Generated project config names real commands and relevant project files.
Persona can state the next Kitsoki command or room to use.
No silent operator-question defaulting occurs.

scenarios.label: Bug fix with hidden oracle
scenarios.task: Start from public issue/repro material, drive Kitsoki's bugfix workflow, and score the resulting worktree with the project's deterministic oracle.

natural_utterances.text: resolve the red gate test that's already written but not committed
file the bug, then land the fixes

success_criteria: Hidden oracle passes.
Full suite passes or failure is classified.
Patch shape is reviewable for the persona's project.

scenarios.label: PRD and design workflow
scenarios.task: Use Kitsoki to turn a project improvement idea into a PRD/design artifact grounded in repository files, conventions, and tests.

natural_utterances.text: i want to dogfood kitsoki to build a critical new feature
what does it take to get an extension uploaded to vs code marketplace?

success_criteria: Artifacts cite real repository files and commands.
Scope is small enough for review.
Open questions are explicit instead of hidden in generated prose.

scenarios.label: Feature implementation
scenarios.task: Use Kitsoki to implement a small accepted design slice and validate it with project tests.
success_criteria: Implementation follows the design artifact.
Tests are targeted and deterministic.
Remaining work is separated from completed work.

scenarios.label: Evidence-backed product bug
scenarios.task: Convert any confusing or broken Kitsoki behavior observed during the journey into a useful product bug report with visual and trace evidence.
success_criteria: Report includes expected versus actual behavior.
Report includes deterministic reproduction context.
Sensitive local data is redacted or explicitly marked local-only.
